dimensions.eval_rule: Evaluate whether the candidate proactively eliminates uncertainty. They must clearly define verifiable CUJs (Critical User Journeys) and establish end-to-end success metrics (e.g., P99 latency, availability SLA). If the candidate rushes to draw diagrams without defining these, mark as a Red Flag.

Check whether the candidate thinks in terms of math and probability. When they propose an architecture or data flow, do they proactively provide capacity estimates based on throughput, storage, bandwidth, or cost? Are the estimates physically plausible?

Do not dictate the tech stack. Only evaluate logical consistency. Do the chosen technologies' characteristics contradict the established CUJs and Metrics? Are there obvious blind spots (e.g., introducing a strong-consistency component while targeting ultra-low latency)?

Evaluate the candidate's trade-off ability. When they propose core components (DB, cache, MQ), check if they proactively compare at least two alternatives with Pros/Cons. 1. If they drop a single solution without comparison, mark negative and set NEEDS_PROBING to guide comparison. 2. If after guidance they still cannot articulate alternatives, set FATAL_FLAW.

Evaluate the candidate's reasoning on scalability, resilience, reliability, and cost. Have they considered disaster recovery, graceful degradation, and rate limiting? Can the design survive extreme conditions?

Probe the candidate's depth on core components (Redis, Kafka, MySQL, OS kernel). Check whether they go beyond API-level usage into storage engines, network models, memory allocation, consensus algorithms, or replication protocols. 1. If they stay at buzzword level, set NEEDS_PROBING for deep-dive follow-up. 2. If after probing they still can't explain internals or make factual errors, set FATAL_FLAW.

Fact-check the candidate's technical claims. For every technology mentioned (MySQL, Redis, Kafka, JVM, locking mechanisms), verify that their described principles, physical characteristics, and use cases match objective reality. 1. If the candidate makes category errors (e.g., claiming MySQL runs on JVM, or Redis supports SQL joins), record immediately as negative signal. 2. Minor slips -> NEEDS_PROBING for clarification; gross violations -> FATAL_FLAW.

Verify data structure and algorithm time complexity fundamentals. If the candidate claims 'a singly linked list can delete itself in O(1)' or 'hash table resizing never blocks', record as negative signal immediately. For LRU specifically, must hear 'HashMap + Doubly Linked List' as the core combination — missing either fails the O(1) requirement. For distributed systems, check understanding of B+ trees, LSM trees, Bloom filters.

Through extreme scenario hypotheticals (100x traffic spike, primary datacenter network partition), probe the candidate's knowledge boundaries and emergency thinking. Evaluate whether they are blindly guessing or can reason from first principles.
Ongoing evaluation throughout: Is the candidate's thinking structured? Is their communication clear? Can they articulate complex system trade-offs concisely?
